title: Smelowskia sisymbrioides
taxonomy: Smelowskia sisymbrioides is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This family comprises a diverse range of plants, including those commonly known as cabbages, mustards, and wild relatives of the cabbage. Smelowskia sisymbrioides is further classified within the genus Smelowskia, which contains several species of flowering plants. The genus Smelowskia is part of the tribe Arabideae, which is a group of plants within the family Brassicaceae. Smelowskia sisymbrioides is native to certain regions of Asia, and its classification reflects its botanical relationships with other plants within the Brassicaceae family.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. side_effects: Smelowskia sisymbrioides, also known as Russian angelica, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating digestive issues and respiratory problems.
